title: Sudden Dyspnea - Tension Pneumothorax
chief_complaint: Sudden sharp left chest pain and severe shortness of breath while playing basketball. Tall thin male. Trachea deviated to right, distended neck veins.

physical_exam.chest: Absent breath sounds left side, hyperresonance to percussion, trachea deviated to right

hidden_data.cxr: Large left pneumothorax with complete lung collapse, mediastinal shift to right, depressed left diaphragm

key_learning_points: Tension PTX = tracheal deviation AWAY + hypotension + distended neck veins
Follow with chest tube (5th ICS MAL)
Do NOT wait for CXR if unstable
Tall thin males at risk for spontaneous PTX

hints.diagnosis: Clinical diagnosis - do not wait for CXR

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Tension pneumothorax is immediately life-threatening.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is tension pneumothorax. Clinical diagnosis - treat immediately.

cxr.comment: Confirms - but don't delay treatment for unstable patients.

feedback.attending_note: Tension PTX: Immediate needle decompression (14G cannula, 2nd ICS MCL), then chest tube (28-32F, 5th ICS MAL). Connect to underwater seal. Pleurodesis if recurrent. No flying for 2-4 weeks after resolution.
